sa1_e_ans_X6_X6.1_(i):
An established long-term insurance company writes a range of without-profits business. The company sells mainly through financial advisers, although sales through its own direct sales force have been growing steadily in recent years and now account for approximately 20% of new regular premium income. For several years the company has been writing substantial volumes of accelerated critical illness insurance policies. A recent crude investigation into the profitability of the in-force business for this contract showed that the business was making a loss. The company has therefore decided to investigate the situation more fully, including an assessment of the financial consequences for the company if the situation were to persist.

sa1_e_ans_201809_Q4_(i):
accept all applications - charge same premium for all ph 
though each insurer's premium may differ

consequences:

- accept all applications:

cannot reject bad risks
high scope of anti selection


-charge same premium:

the extra risk is to be spread among all the remaining ph
so premium rates will go very high

there are only two insurers so the risk has to be shared between them
the overall premium rates will go up for the whole industry
one insurer may cut rates to capture market
this may force the other insurer out of the market

sa1_e_ans_Ch30_30.1_(i):
Whether mortality assmns will change?

Mortality of ph in higher level of care is expected to be higher than those in lower level of care
The initial condition is that the ph in lower level of care will continue in that level and cannot be changed
So the mortality assmn in pricing has to take account of the prob that the ph could move to the higher level of care during the term of the policy
and thereby the mortality assmn will have to adjusted for that level
